executive_summary: 1. OCR ENGINE BROKEN IN PRODUCTION: Neither EasyOCR nor pytesseract are in the main requirements.txt (only in requirements-extras.txt which may not be installed). The log error 'No OCR backend available' confirms this. Every 'read text' request fails silently.

2. SPATIAL PERCEPTION DEFAULTS TO MOCK DETECTORS: YOLO and MiDaS are disabled by default (SPATIAL_USE_YOLO=false, SPATIAL_USE_MIDAS=false), so the system runs MockObjectDetector which returns hardcoded fake detections. Real obstacle detection is non-functional — a critical safety hazard for blind users.

3. STALE-FRAME CACHE IN SPATIAL MOCK: The src/tools/spatial.py MockObjectDetector caches detections by image dimensions and returns stale results for same-sized frames — the system may claim 'Path clear' while obstacles appear, or report ghost objects. The vqa_engine/perception.py mock has the same class but without caching (inconsistency).

4. NO CAMERA WATCHDOG RESTART LOGIC: The Watchdog monitors camera health but never restarts the capture pipeline when camera_stall > 2000ms. It only logs and fires alert callbacks — no automatic recovery, no user-facing spoken alert wired in production.

5. LLM ENDPOINT DEFAULTS TO localhost:11434 (Ollama): The LLM_BASE_URL is hardcoded to 'http://localhost:11434/v1'. In cloud/Docker deployments without Ollama, every LLM call fails. There is no fallback or health check.

6. CI SMOKE TEST DOES NOT VERIFY END-TO-END PIPELINE: The GitHub Actions CI runs pytest unit tests but never starts the Docker container, feeds a synthetic frame, or asserts that /perception/frame or /health returns a valid response. Integration coverage is effectively zero.

root_causes.short_name: OCR backends not installed by default

log_lines: OCR error: No OCR backend available (install easyocr or pytesseract)
config_values: easyocr and pytesseract are only in requirements-extras.txt, not requirements.txt

root_causes.reason: Log explicitly confirms no backend. requirements.txt omits both packages. Docker RUN installs extras with '|| true' which swallows failures.

root_causes.short_name: Spatial perception uses mock detectors — real models disabled
root_causes.reason: Config defaults disable all real ML models. MockObjectDetector returns hardcoded chair/table/person detections. SimpleDepthEstimator returns a linear gradient. No real sensing occurs.

root_causes.short_name: Mock detector caches stale detections by image dimensions
root_causes.reason: If two consecutive frames have the same dimensions (nearly always true for a fixed camera), the mock returns the same cached Detection list, making scene-change detection impossible.

root_causes.short_name: LLM base URL hardcoded to localhost Ollama
root_causes.reason: In Docker/cloud deployment Ollama is absent. No env-var override for LLM_BASE_URL. openai.LLM calls will timeout with ConnectionRefusedError.

root_causes.short_name: Watchdog detects stalls but has no recovery action
root_causes.reason: Watchdog._run_checks fires alert callbacks but no production callback is registered that restarts the capture pipeline or speaks a warning to the user.

root_causes.short_name: YOLO/MiDaS model files not bundled or downloaded
root_causes.reason: Even if SPATIAL_USE_YOLO=true, there is no model file. torch and ultralytics are commented out in requirements.txt. No download script exists.

root_causes.short_name: SimpleDepthEstimator produces synthetic gradient, not real depth
root_causes.reason: estimate_depth() returns np.linspace from max to min depth — a linear vertical gradient regardless of scene content. All distance values reported to the user are fabricated.

root_causes.short_name: No CI smoke test for container + synthetic frame
root_causes.reason: CI builds the Docker image but does not run it. No test sends a frame to /perception/frame or /health.

root_causes.short_name: Face engine enabled by default but no consent flow wired
root_causes.reason: Face engine is enabled but registration is off, so face detection runs but cannot match identities. The /face/consent API exists but is not integrated into the agent session — no prompt asks users for consent before processing starts.

root_causes.short_name: Memory engine has no on-device encryption at rest
impacted_features: Privacy for stored embeddings and RAG data
root_causes.reason: cryptography is in requirements.txt but FACE_ENCRYPTION_ENABLED defaults to false. No encryption is applied to FAISS indexes or memory backups.

root_causes.short_name: QR scanning triggers despite anti-noise rules when QR_AUTO_DETECT=true

log_lines: Agent system prompt says NEVER auto-trigger QR scanning

root_causes.reason: Config enables auto-detect but the system prompt forbids it. No code path actually auto-triggers QR from general vision queries, but the config flag is misleading and could be wired later without noticing the conflict.

root_causes.short_name: Dockerfile uses || true for extras install, masking failures
impacted_features: Docker deployment of OCR, advanced models
root_causes.reason: 'pip install -r requirements-extras.txt || true' silently ignores all install failures. If easyocr or pytesseract fails to install, the container runs without OCR.

root_causes.short_name: Two distinct MockObjectDetector classes with different behaviors
root_causes.reason: spatial.py mock caches by dimensions; perception.py mock does not. Different detection counts and class names. Depending on which pipeline (VQA vs src/tools) handles a frame, results differ.

root_causes.short_name: proactive_cadence_s too aggressive at 2s with debounce window of 5s
root_causes.reason: With mock detectors always returning the same objects, the debouncer suppresses most announcements, but when debounce expires the same stale detection is announced again repeatedly.

root_causes.short_name: No model warm-up — first inference suffers cold-start latency
root_causes.reason: EasyOCR model downloads on first call (~180MB). YOLO/MiDaS load lazily. First user query may take 30+ seconds.

buckets.description: Object detection under varying conditions: lighting, distance, occlusion, motion blur, reflective surfaces, indoor/outdoor, crowds

sample_scenarios.title: Detect single obstacle in well-lit indoor scene
sample_scenarios.expected_behavior: System detects chair 1.5m ahead and announces 'Chair 1.5 meters ahead'
reproduction_steps: Feed 640x480 JPEG of room with single chair at known distance
Assert detection list contains 'chair' with bbox
sample_scenarios.test_data: Synthetic: OpenCV-rendered room scene with single object at calibrated depth

sample_scenarios.title: Detect multiple obstacles in dim lighting
sample_scenarios.expected_behavior: System detects at least top-3 hazards, reports closest first
reproduction_steps: Feed gamma-reduced (dark) synthetic image with 5 objects

sample_scenarios.title: Detect glass door (transparent obstacle)
sample_scenarios.expected_behavior: System warns about glass door ahead even with low contrast
sample_scenarios.acceptance_criterion: Detection of 'door' or 'glass' class, or fallback to depth-based warning
reproduction_steps: Feed real or synthetic image of glass door
sample_scenarios.test_data: Real image dataset: glass door samples from accessibility test sets

sample_scenarios.title: No false positives in empty corridor
sample_scenarios.acceptance_criterion: Zero detections or all detections > 5m, response is 'Path clear'

sample_scenarios.title: Handle camera motion blur during walking
sample_scenarios.expected_behavior: System either detects objects or falls back to 'Proceed with caution'
sample_scenarios.acceptance_criterion: No crash, response within 500ms, either valid detection or safe fallback
reproduction_steps: Apply Gaussian blur kernel (15,15) to synthetic scene
sample_scenarios.test_data: Synthetic: motion-blurred scene at various kernel sizes

buckets.description: Depth accuracy at various ranges, with mono vs stereo, indoor/outdoor, and reflective/transparent surfaces

sample_scenarios.expected_behavior: System reads drug name and dosage aloud

Assert full_text contains expected drug name

Assert some text returned or graceful degradation

buckets.description: STT accuracy with noise, accents, whisper, TTS latency, Deepgram connection failures, ElevenLabs rate limits

sample_scenarios.title: Camera disconnects mid-session

sample_scenarios.acceptance_criterion: Response within 2s containing 'proceed with caution', no hang

generator_algorithm.description: Deterministic scenario generator for expanding to 1000+ scenarios from templates

templates.pattern: Detect {object} at {distance}m in {lighting} with {occlusion} at {angle} degrees
templates.generates: 20 * 8 * 6 * 4 * 5 = 19200 combos; sample 200 via seed=42 hash modulo

templates.pattern: Read {text_type} text in {language} on {surface} under {lighting} at {distance}m

generator_algorithm.sampling_strategy: SHA256(template_id + variable_combo_index + seed) mod N < target_count ÷ total_combos

fixes.title: Fix missing OCR backend — add install + runtime detection + fallback
fixes.description: Move easyocr and pytesseract from requirements-extras.txt to requirements.txt, fix Dockerfile, add runtime probe with spoken fallback.

patches.description: Add OCR backends to main requirements
patches.diff:
--- a/requirements.txt
+++ b/requirements.txt
@@ after 'Pillow>=9.0.0' line @@
+# OCR backends (at least one required for text reading)
+easyocr>=1.7.0
+pytesseract>=0.3.10

patches.description: Remove || true from extras install so failures are visible
patches.diff:
--- a/Dockerfile
+++ b/Dockerfile
@@ -20 @@
-RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt \
-    && pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements-extras.txt || true
+RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt \
+    && pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements-extras.txt

patches.description: Add startup log of OCR backend status
patches.action:
After the TESSERACT_AVAILABLE block, add:

logger.info('OCR backends: easyocr=%s tesseract=%s', EASYOCR_AVAILABLE, TESSERACT_AVAILABLE)
if not EASYOCR_AVAILABLE and not TESSERACT_AVAILABLE:
    logger.error('NO OCR BACKEND AVAILABLE. Install easyocr>=1.7.0 or pytesseract>=0.3.10 + tesseract binary.')

verification_commands: SPATIAL_USE_YOLO=true YOLO_MODEL_PATH=models/yolov8n.onnx python -c "from src.tools.spatial import YOLODetector; d = YOLODetector('models/yolov8n.onnx'); print(d.is_ready())"

fixes.title: Remove dimension-based cache from MockObjectDetector
fixes.description: The mock detector in src/tools/spatial.py caches by (width, height) — remove caching so every frame gets fresh (mock) detections.

patches.action: Remove lines: 'if (width, height) == self._last_dims and self._cached_detections: return self._cached_detections' and 'self._last_dims = (width, height)' and 'self._cached_detections = [...]'. Always compute fresh detections.

fixes.title: Make LLM endpoint configurable via environment variables
fixes.description: Allow LLM_BASE_URL and LLM_API_KEY to be set from env vars.

patches.description: Replace hardcoded LLM URL with env-var lookup

Add lines:

LLM_API_KEY=ollama

patches.diff:

fixes.title: Add model warm-up on startup
fixes.description: Run a dummy inference through each loaded model during entrypoint() to eliminate cold-start latency.

patches.description: After VQA engine init, add warm-up call
patches.code_snippet:
# After: logger.info('VQA Engine initialized...')
try:
    from PIL import Image as PILImage
    dummy = PILImage.new('RGB', (320, 240), color=(128, 128, 128))
    await userdata._vqa_pipeline.process(dummy)
    logger.info('VQA pipeline warm-up complete')
except Exception as e:
    logger.warning('VQA warm-up failed (non-fatal): %s', e)

fixes.title: Wire face consent check into agent session startup
fixes.description: Before face detection processes any frame, check consent status. If not granted, skip face pipeline.

patches.description: In entrypoint, only enable face processing if consent file exists and is true
patches.code_snippet:
# After face engine imports, before processing:
if face_engine_available:
    consent_file = 'data/face_consent.json'
    import json, os
    consent_granted = False
    if os.path.exists(consent_file):
        with open(consent_file) as f:
            consent_granted = json.load(f).get('consent', False)
    if not consent_granted:
        logger.info('Face detection: consent not granted, disabling face pipeline')
        # Don't wire face detector into orchestrator

mitigations.field_behavior: User says 'read this' and gets no response or a cryptic error. Blind user can't read medicine labels.

mitigation.description: 3-tier fallback: EasyOCR → Tesseract → MSER heuristic → spoken install hint

mitigations.field_behavior: User walking, camera freezes. System serves stale frames or goes silent. Dangerous.

mitigation.description: Watchdog detects stall > 2000ms, resets video stream, speaks warning, logs event.
mitigation.code:
# See FIX-02 for full implementation.
# Key logic:
# 1. Reset persistent_stream and cached_video_track
# 2. Speak: 'Camera appears disconnected. Trying to reconnect.'
# 3. If reconnect fails within 5s, speak: 'Camera offline. Please check device.'
# 4. Set a flag to prevent stale-frame queries from returning old data

mitigations.field_behavior: Agent session closes, user hears nothing. No reconnection.

mitigation.description: Wrap agent startup in retry loop (already exists for connect, extend to session recovery).
mitigation.code:
# In entrypoint, add session-close handler:
@ctx.room.on('disconnected')
async def _on_disconnect():
    logger.warning('LiveKit room disconnected')
    # Attempt reconnect
    for attempt in range(3):
        try:
            await asyncio.sleep(2 * (attempt + 1))
            await ctx.connect()
            logger.info('Reconnected to LiveKit room')
            return
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error('Reconnect attempt %d failed: %s', attempt + 1, e)
    logger.error('Failed to reconnect after 3 attempts')

mitigations.field_behavior: User speaks but nothing happens. No error feedback.

mitigation.description: Add STT error handler that speaks 'I didn't catch that. Please try again.' via TTS.

mitigations.field_behavior: Process killed, user abandoned. On edge devices with 512MB-2GB RAM.

mitigation.description: Monitor memory before inference. If >80% used, skip heavy models and fall back to mock/lite.

mitigations.field_behavior: User query hangs for 30s timeout. No response.

mitigation.description: Add 3s timeout to LLM calls. On timeout, return quick-answer from scene graph or static fallback.

test_harness.overview: Reproducible local + CI test harness using Docker Compose, pytest, synthetic data generators
test_harness.docker_compose_yml:
version: '3.8'
services:
  assistant:
    build: .
    ports:
      - '8000:8000'
      - '8081:8081'
    environment:
      - SPATIAL_PERCEPTION_ENABLED=true
      - SPATIAL_USE_YOLO=false
      - ENABLE_QR_SCANNING=true
      - LLM_BASE_URL=http://mock-llm:8080/v1
      - LLM_API_KEY=test
    depends_on:
      - mock-llm
    healthcheck:
      test: ['CMD', 'python', '-c', 'import httpx; r=httpx.get("http://localhost:8000/health"); assert r.status_code==200']
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
  mock-llm:
    image: python:3.11-slim
    command: python -c "from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler; import json; class H(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):\n  def do_POST(self):\n    self.send_response(200)\n    self.send_header('Content-Type','application/json')\n    self.end_headers()\n    self.wfile.write(json.dumps({'choices':[{'message':{'content':'Path clear.'}}]}).encode())\nHTTPServer(('',8080),H).serve_forever()"
    ports:
      - '8080:8080'
test_harness.makefile_target:
test-smoke:
	docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml up -d --build
	sleep 15  # wait for healthcheck
	python -c "
import httpx, json, sys
r = httpx.get('http://localhost:8000/health')
assert r.status_code == 200, f'Health check failed: {r.status_code}'
data = r.json()
print('Health:', json.dumps(data, indent=2))
assert data['status'] == 'ok'
print('SMOKE TEST PASSED')
"
	docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml down

example_pytest_tests.code:
import pytest
import httpx
from PIL import Image
import io
import base64

@pytest.fixture
def synthetic_frame():
    """Generate a 640x480 synthetic room image."""
    import numpy as np
    img = Image.fromarray(np.random.randint(0, 255, (480, 640, 3), dtype=np.uint8))
    return img

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_health_endpoint():
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(base_url='http://localhost:8000') as client:
        r = await client.get('/health')
        assert r.status_code == 200
        data = r.json()
        assert data['status'] == 'ok'

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_ocr_pipeline_available():
    from ocr_engine import OCRPipeline
    pipe = OCRPipeline()
    assert pipe.is_ready, 'OCR pipeline should have at least one backend'

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_perception_returns_detections(synthetic_frame):
    from vqa_engine import create_perception_pipeline
    pipeline = create_perception_pipeline(use_mock=True)
    result = await pipeline.process(synthetic_frame)
    assert len(result.detections) > 0
    assert result.detections[0].class_name in ('person', 'chair', 'table')

import pytest
import asyncio
from watchdog import Watchdog, WatchdogConfig

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_camera_stall_alert():
    alerts = []
    async def mock_alert(comp, msg):
        alerts.append((comp, msg))
    
    wd = Watchdog(WatchdogConfig(
        camera_stall_threshold_ms=100,
        check_interval_ms=50,
    ))
    wd.register_component('camera')
    wd.register_component('orchestrator')
    wd.on_alert(mock_alert)
    
    # Simulate active session
    wd.heartbeat('orchestrator')
    wd.heartbeat('camera')
    
    await wd.start()
    # Wait for stall detection
    await asyncio.sleep(0.3)
    await wd.stop()
    
    assert len(alerts) > 0, 'Should have fired camera stall alert'
    assert alerts[0][0] == 'camera'

import pytest
from ocr_engine.engine import get_ocr_status, get_install_instructions

def test_ocr_status_reports_backends():
    status = get_ocr_status()
    assert 'easyocr_available' in status
    assert 'tesseract_available' in status
    assert 'any_backend_available' in status

def test_install_instructions_not_empty():
    instructions = get_install_instructions()
    assert 'easyocr' in instructions.lower() or 'tesseract' in instructions.lower()

object_detection.primary: YOLOv8n ONNX (6.2 MB, <50ms on CPU, 80+ classes)
object_detection.fallback: YOLOv8-pico or MobileNet-SSD for Raspberry Pi

depth_estimation.fallback: Disparity from stereo if dual-camera available

ocr.fallback: Tesseract 5.x (CPU, fast, lower accuracy on complex layouts)

memory_cpu_recommendations.minimum: 2 CPU cores, 2 GB RAM, no GPU (mock models + Tesseract OCR)
memory_cpu_recommendations.recommended: 4 CPU cores, 4 GB RAM, optional GPU (YOLOv8n ONNX + MiDaS small + EasyOCR)
memory_cpu_recommendations.optimal: 8 CPU cores, 8 GB RAM, GPU (full pipeline + real-time continuous processing)

warm_up_batching.warm_up: Run dummy 320x240 inference through each model at startup (adds ~2s to boot, saves 5-30s on first query)
warm_up_batching.batching: For continuous processing, batch frames: process every 3rd frame at 10 FPS capture → 3.3 inference/sec. Skip intermediate frames with ring buffer.

metrics.name: ally_frame_capture_rate_fps
metrics.description: Live frame capture rate in frames/sec

metrics.name: ally_detection_count
metrics.description: Number of objects detected per frame

metrics.name: ally_missed_hazard_rate
metrics.description: Estimated missed-hazard rate based on frame staleness and detection gaps

metrics.name: ally_camera_stall_total
metrics.description: Camera stall events detected by watchdog

metrics.name: ally_e2e_query_latency_ms
metrics.description: User query to spoken response latency

consent_checks.consent_flow: User must explicitly opt in via voice command ('enable face recognition') or /face/consent API
consent_checks.retention: Embeddings deleted on opt-out. No automatic retention > 30 days unless explicitly configured.
consent_checks.storage: data/face_consent.json stores consent state; embeddings in .npy files are encrypted if FACE_ENCRYPTION_ENABLED=true

consent_checks.retention: Auto-expiry configurable (default 7 days). User can delete all via /memory/delete_all
consent_checks.storage: FAISS index + JSON metadata in data/memory_index/

encryption.at_rest: AES-256 via cryptography library when FACE_ENCRYPTION_ENABLED=true. FAISS indexes currently unencrypted — recommend encrypting.
encryption.in_transit: LiveKit uses DTLS/SRTP. API server should use HTTPS in production (currently HTTP).

onboarding_prompts: Welcome to Ally Vision Assistant. For your privacy: I don't store any personal data by default. Would you like me to remember faces? Say 'yes' to enable, or 'no' to keep it off.
Memory feature: I can remember past conversations to give better answers. This data stays on your device. Say 'enable memory' to turn it on.
You can say 'forget everything' at any time to delete all stored data.
compliance_checklist: GDPR Article 7: Consent clearly obtained before face/memory processing
GDPR Article 17: Right to erasure implemented via /face/forget_all and /memory/delete_all
GDPR Article 20: Data portability — export endpoint for stored data (TODO: implement)
CCPA: Disclosure of data categories collected (face embeddings, conversation history)
ADA/Section 508: Ensure all consent prompts are voice-accessible
No covert recognition: Face embeddings never processed without explicit opt-in
Data minimization: Only store what is needed; auto-expiry on memory entries
Local-first: All data stored on-device by default; cloud sync is opt-in

items.task: FIX-01: Install OCR backends in requirements.txt and Dockerfile
items.safety_impact: HIGH — enables text reading for medicine bottles, signs

items.task: FIX-02: Wire watchdog alert callback with camera restart + spoken warning
items.safety_impact: CRITICAL — prevents silent failures when camera disconnects

items.task: FIX-05: Remove stale-frame cache from MockObjectDetector
FIX-06: Make LLM_BASE_URL configurable via env var
FIX-07: Remove || true from Dockerfile extras install
FIX-04: Add model download script + auto-detect YOLO/MiDaS
items.safety_impact: CRITICAL — replaces mock detectors with real perception

items.task: FIX-03: CI smoke test with Docker container + synthetic frame
FIX-08: MiDaS depth auto-enable when model present
FIX-09: Model warm-up at startup
FIX-10: Wire face consent into session startup
Implement Prometheus metrics exporter
Add structured JSON logging throughout pipeline
Replace all mock detectors with validated real models (YOLOv8n + MiDaS + optional SAM-lite)
Implement full 1000+ scenario test suite with synthetic data generators
Add on-device encryption for all stored data (FAISS, memories, embeddings)
Implement data portability export endpoint (GDPR Article 20)
